block-definition.comment: Put this at the TOP of your drawing script. Snap! paints pen trails as pixels unless it is told to keep them as vectors as well - this switches that on and empties the trail, so what you draw next can be sent to Sangala Studio as lines a machine can follow. (It is the same setting as "Log pen vectors" in Snap!'s settings menu.)

block.l: StageMorph.prototype.enablePenLogging = true; var stage = world.children[0].stage; stage.trailsLog = []; return null;

block-definition.comment: Sends what the turtle drew to Sangala Studio, which puts it on the mat. Sangala Studio must be running (SangalaStudio.exe). THE PEN COLOUR IS THE INSTRUCTION: a BLUE stroke becomes a fold line (perforated); a stroke of any other colour becomes a cut line - or a drawn line, if there is a pen in the tool holder instead of a blade. Nothing moves on its own: a human still presses Make It in Sangala Studio.

block.l: var stage = world.children[0].stage; if (!stage.trailsLog.length) { throw new Error('No pen vectors yet. Put "log pen vectors for Sangala" at the top of your script, then run your drawing.'); } var svg = stage.trailsLogAsPolySVG().src; var x = new XMLHttpRequest(); try { x.open('POST', 'http://127.0.0.1:8787/snap/svg', false); x.send(svg); } catch (e) { throw new Error('Cannot reach Sangala Studio. Is SangalaStudio.exe running? Snap! must also be opened from http://extensions.snap.berkeley.edu (plain http), because a secure page may not talk to a local program.'); } if (x.status !== 200) { throw new Error('Sangala Studio answered ' + x.status); } return null;
